behaviors.description: Renamed files are reported only in the renamed category, not double-counted as deleted+added.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Real failure boundary is CLI↔git subprocess↔repo history. Rename detection depends on actual git behavior/flags, not just local parsing logic. MOCK-HIDE/REAL-INFRA: a mocked git response can hide bugs where the CLI invokes git without rename detection or with wrong diff options. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Integration): rejected because the behavior text is about analyzing a real repository’s changes, and incorrect subprocess invocation would not be caught with in-process fakes. Higher-level rejection: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (Python git CLI, no AI/ML output dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it, then run the CLI as a subprocess against that repo. Assert stdout report has rename count 1 and add/delete counts unchanged for that file path pair.

behaviors.description: Binary-file diffs do not crash the tool, and binary files are counted.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Failure often appears only with real git diff output/encoding from binary content through subprocess pipes. MOCK-HIDE: fixture strings may miss decode/stream edge cases that crash in real execution. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Integration): rejected because the behavior explicitly concerns binary diffs produced by git from a real repo, which can involve subprocess output and OS I/O details. Higher-level rejection: Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no model calls).
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit baseline, then add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success, no traceback, and report includes binary file in appropriate count/category.

behaviors.description: A repository with no commits yields an empty report, not an error.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: This depends on real git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repositories (exit codes/messages) via subprocess. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git success responses can miss real fatal-path handling. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Integration): rejected because the key failure mode is interaction with real git process state in an actual no-commit repo. Higher-level rejection: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (deterministic CLI + git).
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run CLI subprocess, assert successful completion and structurally valid empty report on stdout.

behaviors.description: Reported counts match the repository’s true change history.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: End correctness depends on real git history traversal and subprocess command semantics across commits. Integration with mocked history can miss range/ordering/revision-spec bugs. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Integration): rejected because behavior text requires agreement with the actual repository history, which is exactly the real infra boundary. Higher-level rejection: Agentic/Workflow disqualified (no AI/ML judgment).
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with controlled adds/deletes/renames/modifies across multiple commits, run CLI subprocess, and compare reported category counts to independently computed expected counts from scripted git operations.

behaviors.description: Output is identical when analyzing staged versus unstaged versions of the same changes.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: This behavior targets git index vs working-tree semantics, which are real git state-machine behaviors exposed through subprocess calls. MOCK-HIDE: fakes often collapse staged/unstaged distinctions and would miss command-target bugs. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Integration): rejected because only a real repo/index/worktree setup validates equivalence. Higher-level rejection: Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no LLM-dependent decision steps).
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create identical content change twice: once staged and once unstaged in controlled runs. Execute CLI subprocess for each mode and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
